If the band breaks up (we hope not!) or you simply no longer need to use BandMath, you can cancel your subscription and export your data at any time.
BandMath partners with Stripe to securely handle all billing and subscription data. This ensures that your credit card information is never stored directly on BandMath servers.

BandMath offers different subscription tiers to fit the needs of your tour. Whether you just need the core financial ledger, or you want to unlock advanced features like the Merch Analyzer, managing your subscription is easy.

Nightly merch settlements are notoriously difficult. BandMath's venue split logic automatically calculates the venue's cut of gross (or net) sales, subtracts it from the night's revenue, and accurately splits the remaining profit among the band members.

Bands can designate a "VAT Bank" member. The app automatically calculates and siphons the appropriate tax percentage out of the profit pool and safely parks it in the VAT Bank for government remittance, preventing bands from accidentally spending tax money.
Easily record income (anything from tips to payouts), expenses (like gas, hotels, or gear), and money transfers between members. Full editing capabilities ensure the ledger is always accurate.

The backend automatically splits income and expenses equally among active members and it dynamically handles rounding errors (e.g., splitting $10.00 three ways) by seamlessly assigning leftover pennies to members in a rotating fashion, guaranteeing perfectly balanced books down to the cent.
The app continually calculates pairwise debts (who owes who exactly what) so that bands can settle up instantly without any manual math.
Upload and attach receipt images directly to any transaction, ensuring you always have a digital paper trail.

The app works quietly in the background to solve the complex Debt Settlement problem, continually finding the absolute simplest way for everyone to settle up with the minimum number of money transfers.
Anyone can pay for anything and automatically debit any combination of the other members. Similarly, anyone can take a payment (like a venue payout) and then credit the other members.

A dedicated button instantly downloads a deduplicated, clean CSV of all fan emails collected during the POS checkout. Bands can seamlessly upload this directly into Mailchimp, Klaviyo, or any other marketing tool.

A one-click button downloads the entire financial history into a perfectly formatted, spreadsheet-ready CSV. It includes every detail (Descriptions, Tips, Individual Splits, Payment Methods, and Receipt URLs) so accountants can do their jobs effortlessly.

BandMath locks in the "Cost Goods Sold" (COGS) at the time of purchase. If a band buys a batch of shirts for $5 each, and a second batch for $7 each, the app calculates profit using the exact cost of the specific item sold.
Create custom merch items (e.g., "Tour Tee", "Vinyl") and manage infinite size variations (S, M, L, XL, etc.).
Every time a cart is checked out in the POS, the inventory count for those specific items and sizes is instantly and automatically deducted.
View high-level metrics like Gross Revenue, Total Profit, Average Order Value, and Total Items Sold.
The backend flags items that are running dangerously low on stock or have completely sold out, allowing bands to re-order before losing out on potential revenue.
The app automatically analyzes sales velocity and awards "Trophies" to the highest-grossing items (e.g., "Peony T-shirt - M is your top earner!").
Seamlessly toggle between Cash and Card checkouts to keep an accurate physical cash box count while tracking digital revenue.
Fan emails can be optionally captured during checkout for digital receipts or mailing list signups.
Apply custom discounts to total orders or accept tips from generous fans. The backend automatically distributes tip revenue directly into the band's profit pool.
Easily add multiple products and sizes to a cart.
Welcome to BandMath! Setting up your band is the first step toward gaining total financial clarity for your tours and merchandise.
BandMath is built for collaboration. To get the most out of the platform, you should invite your bandmates, tour managers, and merch sellers to join your band's workspace.
Ready to hit the road? Here is a quick 5-minute walkthrough of the core BandMath loop to get you started on your first tour.

Last Updated: 2026-06-27
Because BandMath is used by touring bands around the world, you can easily customize the app to fit your region.
BandMath keeps you informed about important financial events in your band's workspace.
BandMath uses a simple but strict role-based permission system to ensure your financial data remains secure and organized. There are two primary roles in any band workspace: Admins and Members.
If you've been operating as a Shadow Profile and you finally decide to download the BandMath app, you can easily "claim" your profile to view your balances and log your own transactions.

BandMath's architecture is designed around a unique concept called Shadow Profiles. This feature exists because we understand the reality of touring bands: not everyone wants to download a finance app.
The Scenario:
The Scenario:
The Scenario:
If the band breaks up (we hope not!) or you simply no longer need to use BandMath, you can cancel your subscription and export your data at any time.

To stop future billing: Navigate to Band Settings > Billing. Tap Manage Subscription to open the Stripe portal. Select Cancel Plan. Your premium features will remain active until the end of your current billing cycle. After that, your workspace will be downgraded to the free tier. Your data is not deleted when you cancel a premium subscription.

We believe your financial data belongs to you. You can export your entire ledger to a CSV file for your accountant or for your own records. Go to the Settings page. Select Data Export. Choose the date range you wish to export, and tap Download CSV. The generated CSV will contain every transaction, including the date, amount, payer, receivers, and itemized merch sales.

If you want to permanently erase your band's data from our servers: [!CAUTION] Deleting your band workspace is irreversible. All ledger history, merch inventory, and member profiles will be permanently erased. Go to Band Settings. Scroll to the very bottom and tap Delete Band Workspace. You will be prompted to type the name of your band to confirm the deletion. Only an Admin can delete a band workspace.
BandMath partners with Stripe to securely handle all billing and subscription data. This ensures that your credit card information is never stored directly on BandMath servers.

If your credit card has expired, or you simply want to switch the billing to a different card, you can do so through the Stripe Customer Portal. Navigate to the Band Settings page in the BandMath app. Tap on the Billing tab. Tap Manage Subscription. This will open a secure browser window to the Stripe Customer Portal. Under the Payment Methods section, you can add a new card, delete an old card, or set a new default payment method.

If a recurring subscription payment fails, Stripe will automatically attempt to retry the charge a few days later. You will receive an email notification prompting you to update your payment method. If the payment continues to fail after several retries, your band workspace will automatically be downgraded to the free/core tier until the payment issue is resolved.

BandMath offers different subscription tiers to fit the needs of your tour. Whether you just need the core financial ledger, or you want to unlock advanced features like the Merch Analyzer, managing your subscription is easy.

You can view the current available tiers and their features by navigating to the Billing tab in your Band Settings. Core Ledger: Basic transaction tracking, automated debt settlement, and basic team management. Merch Manager: Unlocks the Point-of-Sale (POS) system, advanced inventory tracking, and deep insights from the Merch Analyzer.

To upgrade your band's workspace: Navigate to the Band Settings page. Select Billing. Choose the tier you want to upgrade to and tap Subscribe. You will be redirected to our secure Stripe checkout portal to enter your payment details. Once the payment is successful, BandMath will automatically unlock the premium features for your entire band workspace. Note that only one subscription is required per band, not per user! [!WARNING] Only Admins have the permission to change the band's subscription tier. If you are a Member and need to upgrade, please contact your Admin.

If your tour is over and you no longer need the advanced features, you can downgrade your workspace at any time. Go to the Billing tab in Band Settings. Select Manage Subscription. You will be taken to the Stripe customer portal where you can select a lower tier. Your premium features will remain active until the end of your current billing cycle, after which you will be moved to the downgraded tier. Your historical data from the higher tier (like past merch sales) will be preserved, but you may lose access to the advanced analytics dashboards.

Welcome to BandMath! Setting up your band is the first step toward gaining total financial clarity for your tours and merchandise.

When you first sign up for BandMath, you will be prompted to create your Band Profile. This profile acts as the central hub for all of your band's financial data, merchandise inventory, and team members.

During setup, you will be asked to select your band's base currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). [!IMPORTANT] The base currency is the primary currency used for all ledger calculations, settlements, and reporting. While you can log transactions in other currencies, BandMath will always reconcile your final balances in your chosen base currency.

Upon creating your band, BandMath automatically creates two special "members" in your roster: The Band Bank: Represents the communal pool of money owned by the band as an entity (e.g., your actual shared bank account or a physical cash box). The VAT Bank: Used exclusively for setting aside value-added tax (VAT) or sales tax collected during merch sales. These banks are crucial for the double-entry accounting system and cannot be deleted.
Once your band profile is created, you automatically become the Admin. Your next step is to start building out your roster by inviting your band members.
BandMath is built for collaboration. To get the most out of the platform, you should invite your bandmates, tour managers, and merch sellers to join your band's workspace.

As an Admin, you can invite new members to your band by generating an Invite Code. Navigate to the Band Settings page. Select Manage Members. Click Add Member and enter their name and role. An invite code will be automatically generated. Share this 6-character code with your bandmate.

When your bandmate downloads the BandMath app, they will create their own user account. On the welcome screen, they will select Join Existing Band and enter the 6-character invite code you provided. This securely links their user account to your band's workspace and grants them access to view the ledger and log transactions.

We know that not everyone in the band will want to download a finance app (we're looking at you, drummers!). BandMath solves this with Shadow Profiles. When you add a member to the roster, they are initially created as a Shadow Profile. This means they exist in the ledger so you can track their shares, debts, and payouts, even if they never download the app. If a member does eventually download the app and uses their invite code, their account is instantly bound to their Shadow Profile, and they gain access to all their historical transaction data. For more details on member permissions, see Admins vs. Members.
documents.s: Shadow Profiles (For Members Without the App)

documents.t: Ready to hit the road? Here is a quick 5-minute walkthrough of the core BandMath loop to get you started on your first tour.
documents.s: The First Tour: A Quick Walkthrough

documents.t: Before you can sell merchandise, you need to tell BandMath what you have in stock. Navigate to the Merch Inventory page. Add your products (e.g., T-Shirts, Vinyl, CDs) and specify the cost to produce them and the retail price. BandMath will automatically track your inventory levels as you log sales.

When you're at the merch table after a show: Open the Point-of-Sale (POS) tab. Tap the items the fan wants to buy to add them to the cart. Select the payment method (Cash or Card). Hit Checkout. BandMath instantly records the revenue, deducts the items from your inventory, and calculates the profit.

Did someone buy gas for the van? Go to the Transactions page. Tap Add Transaction -> Expense. Select who paid for the gas, and select the band members who should split the cost in the who owes section. BandMath automatically splits the expense and updates everyone's internal balances.

At the end of the night (or the end of the tour), navigate to the Standings page. Here, you'll see the magic of BandMath's Automated Debt Settlement. Instead of a tangled web of "who owes who," BandMath calculates the absolute simplest way for everyone to settle up using the minimum number of money transfers.
BandMath uses some specific terminology to handle the complexities of tour accounting. If you're ever confused by a term in the app, you can reference this glossary.
A band member who has downloaded the BandMath app, created an account, and linked it to the band workspace using an Invite Code.

BandMath's core algorithm. Instead of creating a complex web of "who owes who" based on individual transactions, the algorithm analyzes the net balances of everyone in the band and outputs the absolute simplest way to settle all debts with the fewest number of cash transfers.

A special, permanent member profile created automatically for every band. It represents the communal pool of money owned by the band as a business entity (e.g., your shared business checking account or the physical cash box in the van).

Our advanced profit-splitting algorithm. When an expense or income is split among multiple members, the division rarely works out to clean integers (e.g., $10.00 split 3 ways is $3.33333...). The Penny Catcher ensures that fractions of a cent are never lost or infinitely rounded, guaranteeing that the ledger balances perfectly to the penny.
A member profile created by an Admin to track the finances of someone who has not (or will not) download the BandMath app. The Admin logs transactions on their behalf.

A special, permanent member profile used exclusively to hold Value-Added Tax (VAT) or sales tax. When you sell merch, the tax portion of the sale is automatically transferred to the VAT Bank, ensuring you don't accidentally spend money that you owe to the government.
Last Updated: 2026-06-27 Welcome to the definitive feature guide for BandMath. Here's a full list of everything BandMath can do to help automate the hardest parts of touring finance.

At its core, BandMath operates on a custodial-style accounting model designed to simplify the reality of shared band expenses. Shared Expenses & Credits: Anyone can pay for anything and automatically debit any combination of the other members. Similarly, anyone can take a payment (like a venue payout) and then credit the other members. Automated Debt Settlement: The app works quietly in the background to solve the complex Debt Settlement problem, continually finding the absolute simplest way for everyone to settle up with the minimum number of money transfers.

At the heart of BandMath is a robust, dynamic ledger designed specifically for the unique financial realities of touring bands and independent artists. Add, Modify, and Delete Transactions: Easily record income (anything from tips to payouts), expenses (like gas, hotels, or gear), and money transfers between members. Full editing capabilities ensure the ledger is always accurate. Smart Receipt Capture: Upload and attach receipt images directly to any transaction, ensuring you always have a digital paper trail. Advanced Profit Splitting ("The Penny Catcher"): The backend automatically splits income and expenses equally among active members and it dynamically handles rounding errors (e.g., splitting $10.00 three ways) by seamlessly assigning leftover pennies to members in a rotating fashion, guaranteeing perfectly balanced books down to the cent. Member Balances & Settlements: The app continually calculates pairwise debts (who owes who exactly what) so that bands can settle up instantly without any manual math.

BandMath eliminates the need for complex, messy spreadsheets by automating the hardest parts of touring finance. VAT Bank Integration: Bands can designate a "VAT Bank" member. The app automatically calculates and siphons the appropriate tax percentage out of the profit pool and safely parks it in the VAT Bank for government remittance, preventing bands from accidentally spending tax money. The Venue Split Calculator: Nightly merch settlements are notoriously difficult. BandMath's venue split logic automatically calculates the venue's cut of gross (or net) sales, subtracts it from the night's revenue, and accurately splits the remaining profit among the band members.

A built-in, lightning-fast digital storefront designed for the chaos of the merch table. Dynamic Cart System: Easily add multiple products and sizes to a cart. Discounts & Tips: Apply custom discounts to total orders or accept tips from generous fans. The backend automatically distributes tip revenue directly into the band's profit pool. Cash & Card Tracking: Seamlessly toggle between Cash and Card checkouts to keep an accurate physical cash box count while tracking digital revenue. Customer Email Capture: Fan emails can be optionally captured during checkout for digital receipts or mailing list signups.

Know exactly what is in the trailer at all times. Product & Size Management: Create custom merch items (e.g., "Tour Tee", "Vinyl") and manage infinite size variations (S, M, L, XL, etc.). Real-time Stock Depletion: Every time a cart is checked out in the POS, the inventory count for those specific items and sizes is instantly and automatically deducted. Historical Cost Tracking: BandMath locks in the "Cost Goods Sold" (COGS) at the time of purchase. If a band buys a batch of shirts for $5 each, and a second batch for $7 each, the app calculates profit using the exact cost of the specific item sold.

A powerful, automated reporting dashboard that acts as the band's virtual merch manager. Profit & ROI Tracking: View high-level metrics like Gross Revenue, Total Profit, Average Order Value, and Total Items Sold. "Trophy" Awards (Top Performers): The app automatically analyzes sales velocity and awards "Trophies" to the highest-grossing items (e.g., "Peony T-shirt - M is your top earner!"). Restock Warnings: The backend flags items that are running dangerously low on stock or have completely sold out, allowing bands to re-order before losing out on potential revenue.

Bands own their data. BandMath makes it easy to plug into the broader music industry ecosystem. Ledger Export to CSV: A one-click button downloads the entire financial history into a perfectly formatted, spreadsheet-ready CSV. It includes every detail (Descriptions, Tips, Individual Splits, Payment Methods, and Receipt URLs) so accountants can do their jobs effortlessly. Email Contact List Export: A dedicated button instantly downloads a deduplicated, clean CSV of all fan emails collected during the POS checkout. Bands can seamlessly upload this directly into Mailchimp, Klaviyo, or any other marketing tool.
Because BandMath is used by touring bands around the world, you can easily customize the app to fit your region.

When you first create your band workspace, you will be prompted to select a Base Currency. [!WARNING] The base currency is a permanent setting for your workspace. Once your first transaction is logged, the base currency cannot be changed. This ensures that historical financial data and settlements remain perfectly accurate. The base currency is the primary currency used for all: Ledger calculations Debt settlements and payouts Merch inventory valuations Profit and loss reporting

BandMath automatically formats your financial inputs and displays based on your selected base currency. This means that if you choose Euro (€) as your base currency, the app will automatically inject the € symbol into input fields like the merch inventory pricing, preventing typos and ensuring a clean user experience. If you notice a currency symbol is missing or incorrect, please reach out to support.
BandMath keeps you informed about important financial events in your band's workspace.

Currently, BandMath relies primarily on in-app alerts and transactional emails (such as payment receipts or subscription warnings from Stripe). If a recurring subscription payment fails, you will receive an email from our billing provider to the email address associated with the Admin account.

We are actively developing push notifications to alert you when: You are added to a transaction by another member Your band's inventory levels drop below a critical threshold (The Merch Analyzer currently provides these warnings in-app, but soon they will be pushed to your phone). A settlement period has closed. Stay tuned to our blog for updates on these features!
BandMath uses a simple but strict role-based permission system to ensure your financial data remains secure and organized. There are two primary roles in any band workspace: Admins and Members.

The user who creates the band profile is automatically assigned as the first Admin. Admins have full control over the band's workspace. Their capabilities include: Managing Members: Adding new members, generating invite codes, and removing members. Managing Settings: Changing the band's name, base currency, and other core settings. Managing Billing: Upgrading or downgrading the band's subscription tier. Full Transaction Control: Admins can edit, void, or delete any transaction in the ledger, regardless of who created it. Merch Management: Full access to add, edit, and delete merchandise inventory and product definitions.

Members are the standard users within a band workspace. They have the ability to participate in the financial ecosystem but with restricted administrative access. Members can: Log Transactions: Add new expenses, incomes, or transfers to the ledger. View the Ledger: See all transactions and the current standings/settlements. Sell Merchandise: Use the Point-of-Sale (POS) system to log merch sales. Members cannot: Delete or edit transactions created by other people. Remove other members from the band. Change the band's core settings or billing tier. [!TIP] A band can have multiple Admins. An existing Admin can promote any Member to an Admin role from the Band Settings page.
If you've been operating as a Shadow Profile and you finally decide to download the BandMath app, you can easily "claim" your profile to view your balances and log your own transactions.

Every Shadow Profile automatically generates a unique 6-character Invite Code. This code acts as a secure key to bind your new user account to the existing financial data your band has been tracking for you. To get your invite code, ask your band's Admin. They can find it next to your name on the Manage Members screen in the Band Settings.

Download the BandMath app from the App Store or Google Play. Open the app and create a new user account (you can use email, Google, or Apple Sign-In). On the welcome screen, select Join Existing Band. Enter the 6-character Invite Code provided by your Admin. [!IMPORTANT] Once you enter the invite code, your new user account is permanently bound to the Shadow Profile. You will instantly inherit all past transactions, debts, and credits associated with that profile, and your Admin will no longer need to track your finances on your behalf.

BandMath's architecture is designed around a unique concept called Shadow Profiles. This feature exists because we understand the reality of touring bands: not everyone wants to download a finance app. Whether it's a stubborn drummer or a temporary hired musician, you still need to track their financial shares and debts accurately.

When an Admin adds a new member to the band's roster, BandMath creates a "Shadow Profile" in the database. This profile has a name and a role, but it is not yet attached to a real user account (in technical terms, their user_id is null). Because the Shadow Profile exists in your band's ledger, you can immediately start assigning transactions to them. You can log that the Shadow Profile paid for gas, or that they owe a split of the hotel bill. BandMath will calculate their balances and settlements perfectly, just as it would for an active user.

Active Profiles: These are members who have downloaded the BandMath app, created an account, and joined the band using an invite code. They can log in and see their own balances on their phone. Shadow Profiles: These are placeholders in your ledger. The Admin tracks their finances on their behalf.

If a member with a Shadow Profile decides they do want to download the app, they can easily claim their profile and instantly access all their historical data. See Claiming a Profile for instructions on how this process works.

The Scenario: A band member wants to buy one of the band's shirts for themselves, but they only want to pay the wholesale cost (e.g., the shirt costs $5 to print, but retails for $25). They want to take the shirt, ensure the inventory stays accurate, and properly reimburse the rest of the band for the wholesale cost. The Nightmare: In a spreadsheet, you'd have to manually deduct the inventory, calculate the cost basis, and then somehow inject a $5 debt for that specific member without inflating your gross retail sales numbers. The BandMath Solution (The "At Cost" POS Checkout): Because BandMath handles inventory and double-entry routing seamlessly, this is processed exactly like a normal fan sale, but utilizing the built-in "At Cost" toggle. Go to the Point of Sale (POS) and add the shirt to the cart. Under the Merch Total, toggle the pricing selector from Retail to At Cost. This automatically reduces the cart total to match the exact wholesale cost of the items. Tap Cash, ensure the active band member is set to the person taking the shirt, and tap Go! to checkout. Note: No physical cash actually needs to change hands. Why this works flawlessly: Inventory & Analytics: The POS system automatically deducts the shirt from inventory and logs the sale at $5. This ensures the Merch Analyzer's ROI and profit margins remain 100% accurate (it doesn't look like a shirt was "lost" or stolen). The Settlement Routing: The algorithm registers that the band member "collected" $5 in cash. Since no actual cash was put into a lockbox, that member is personally holding the $5. The algorithm will automatically split that $5 revenue among the band, meaning the member will effectively "owe" the rest of the band out of their share, perfectly reimbursing the group for the cost of the shirt!

The Scenario: A band member was rushing at the merch table and accidentally checked out the wrong size or applied the wrong discount. They realize the mistake a few minutes later and need to fix it. The Nightmare: In most systems, deleting a sale destroys the financial record, but leaves the inventory permanently skewed. You would have to manually go back into the stock room, figure out exactly which items were logged in that specific cart, and manually add them back to your inventory counts one by one. The BandMath Solution (The Self-Healing Ledger): BandMath's database is deeply interconnected. To fix a bad merch sale, you simply delete it. Go to the Transaction Feed (the Ledger). Locate the incorrect Merch Sale and tap Delete. Re-ring the customer correctly in the POS. Why this works flawlessly: When you hit "Delete" on a Merch Sale, the backend triggers a cascading self-heal: The Math Realignment: The primary transaction and all associated profit splits are instantly deleted, perfectly resetting every band member's standings back to where they were before the sale. The Auto-Restock: The backend opens up the associated order, looks at every single item and size that was in the cart, and automatically adds those exact quantities back into your active inventory. The Analytics: The order records are purged, meaning your Merch Analyzer's ROI, velocity, and "Trophy" metrics remain completely uncontaminated by the mistake.

The Scenario: A band records a full night's worth of merch transactions using the Point-of-Sale system. At the end of the night, they realize their band's tax rate was set incorrectly. As a result, the VAT Bank has collected either too much or too little of the profit pool across dozens of transactions. The Nightmare: In a traditional spreadsheet, the band would have to manually edit every single line item and recalculate the split for every band member. The BandMath Solution ("The Grand Adjustment"): Bands do not need to delete a single transaction or mess with their merch inventory. Because of the double-entry routing algorithm, they can fix the entire night in seconds with a single manual transaction. First, calculate the total discrepancy (e.g., "We collected $100 for tax, but it should have been $150. We are short $50").

If the band is short $50 in tax, it means the band members accidentally pocketed $50 of profit that rightfully belongs to the government. Go to the ledger and click Add Transaction. Create a manual Expense for $50 and title it "VAT Correction". Set the "Paid By" field to the VAT Bank. Result: The algorithm instantly registers that the VAT Bank covered a $50 expense for the group. It automatically deducts the respective split from each active member's standings and routes that money directly into the VAT Bank's balance.
documents.s: Scenario A: The VAT was too LOW (Short $50)

documents.t: If the VAT bank accidentally captured $50 too much, it means the VAT Bank is holding $50 of the band's rightful profit. Go to the ledger and click Add Transaction. Create a manual Income for $50 and title it "VAT Refund". Set the "Collected By" field to the VAT Bank. Result: The algorithm sees that the VAT Bank just collected $50 of band income. It instantly forces the VAT Bank to surrender that money and evenly distributes it back into the band members' balances.
documents.s: Scenario B: The VAT was too HIGH (Overpaid $50)
